subject: FW: Nadia's Apartment - design changes
body:
Please advise

 

Rich Kahn

New York Strategy Group LLC

457 Madison Avenue

Fourth Floor

New York, NY 10022

tel  212-891-6430

fax 212-750-2408

rkahn@nysgmail.com

________________________________

From: Alan A. Barr [mailto:abarr@zeffdesign.com] 
Sent: Friday, December 07, 2007 2:59 PM
To: Rich Kahn
Cc: Mark Zeff; Susana Simonpietri
Subject: RE: Naida's Apartment - design changes

 

Rich,

I just spoke with Mark - he was on a plane before and called in when he
landed to weigh in on the situation. He wanted me to clarify that for
the $20k fee we will redesign and specify under the new color palate,
then turn over the documents to Juan Pablo or your team for project
completion. At that point we will be stepping out of the project. As I
mentioned before, this was to be an easy 1-2-3 project and has turned
out to be otherwise. This has gone on much too long, we have no control
over the situation and see no end in sight. As you have engaged this
other designer it appears that we are obsolete. Thanks for adding this
to the previous clarification.

Regards,
Alan

_____________________________________________
From: Alan A. Barr
Sent: Friday, December 07, 2007 11:37 AM
To: 'Rich Kahn'
Cc: Susana Simonpietri; Mark Zeff
Subject: Naida's Apartment - design changes

Rich,

Per our conversation today I am drafting the enclosed to summarize the
design changes at Nadia's project. Yesterday Susana, our project
manager, had a meeting onsite with Jeffrey, Nadia and Juan Pablo
Molyneux (the color expert). The result of the meeting was that the
entire color palate for the apartment is to change and head in a new
direction. The result is that we need to redesign all material colors
and types (in some cases). Zeff will need to put together a new
presentation for Jeffrey and Nadia to review and approve. From there we
will need to produce new drawings for all the vendors and manufacturers
involved to understand the changes. In addition, we will need to order
new material for all fabrics, leathers, materials, etc that have
changed.

Obviously, this new exercise is a deviation from the approved design
which in most cases is imminently to be completed. This is resulting in
extra time and service beyond our agreement - at the onset the project
was to be a simple 1-2-3 project. As discussed Zeff will need a small
fee of $20k to orchestrate this additional service.

As an alternative, we have no issues if you want Juan Pablo to design
and specify the changes. We would provide him with vendor contacts and
he could document, specify, order and deliver all necessary fabrics,
leathers, etc. He would also need to issue complete specifications to
the GC for all millwork and paint material & color changes. This option
would result in no change from Zeff Design.

Please advise how you would like to proceed. As always I am available to
discuss should you have any questions on the enclosed.

Regards,

Alan

 

Alan Barr

Vice President

abarr@zeffdesign.com <mailto:abarr@zeffdesign.com> 

 

zeffdesign

 

515 West 20th St, 4W

New York,  NY  10011

 

P. 212.580.7090 x202

F. 212.580.7181

www.zeffdesign.com

The result is that we need to redesign all material colors and types (in some cases). Zeff will need to put together a new presentation for Jeffrey and Nadia to review and approve. Obviously, this new exercise is a deviation from the approved design which in most cases is imminently to be completed. Please advise how you would like to proceed. As always I am available to discuss should you have any questions on the enclosed.
